URW: Negative Consequences for the West

URW: Negative Consequences for the West

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western media for its reporting, claiming it prolongs the conflict and causes harm, which aligns with discrediting Western institutions and their role.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing... Another must listen to interview with information that you are missing from the corporation propaganda western media
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text explicitly labels Western media coverage as 'propaganda' and frames it as providing incomplete or false information, directly undermining trust in these media sources.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: New Sarah Westall & Scott Ritter: Russia is Winning
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The title and premise of the content positively highlights Russia's position in the war, framing it as the winning side, which is a form of praise for Russia's military actions.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion..
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement criticizes and discredits Western (specifically American) foreign policy and perception, portraying it as irrational and fear-mongering towards Russia and China.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: time they took responsibility (rather than consequences) of what their politicians are doing in their name..
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This implies that Western populations will face negative consequences ('consequences') for the actions of their politicians, highlighting an adverse outcome for Western societies.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote explicitly evokes fear and anxiety about the potential for nuclear war, presenting it as a seemingly imminent or desired outcome to end suffering, which amplifies war-related fears.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement strongly discredits Western governance by attacking 'un-elected bureaucrats' as representing the worst of humanity, criticizing the structure and morality of Western political systems.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western media for allegedly misrepresenting the war's progress, framing it as a harmful act that prolongs conflict and causes death, which fits the definition of discrediting Western institutions.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Another must listen to interview with information that you are missing from the corporation propaganda western media
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement explicitly labels Western media as 'corporation propaganda,' directly undermining its reliability and integrity, which is the core of the 'Distrust towards Media' narrative.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This question raises the specter of nuclear weapon use, evoking fear and anxiety about a severe escalation of the conflict, which aligns with amplifying war-related fears.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement, though presented in a fatalistic tone, explicitly discusses and normalizes the concept of a full nuclear war, which serves to amplify fears about catastrophic outcomes.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This is a severe and derogatory criticism targeting Americans (as a stand-in for the West), attacking their character and moral standing, which fits the narrative of discrediting the West.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement highlights negative outcomes (being the 'worst of humanity') attributed to Western bureaucrats, predicting or reporting adverse moral consequences for Western governance structures.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: NOBODY asked their populations ..we are just told…but we deserve EVERYTHING we get
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This implies that populations are being acted upon without consent by unaccountable powers ('un-elected bureaucrats'), suggesting a hidden, undemocratic control scheme over Western societies related to the war context.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion..
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western (specifically American) foreign policy as being based on fear-mongering and exaggeration regarding Russia and China, portraying Western actions as irrational and aggressive.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This is a severe and derogatory attack on the character of Americans, aiming to discredit and dehumanize the population of a Western nation.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement attacks the legitimacy and morality of Western governance, framing unelected officials as malevolent actors representing the worst of humanity.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This directly accuses Western media of presenting a false narrative about the war's progress, undermining its credibility and suggesting its reporting is harmful.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Another must listen to interview with information that you are missing from the corporation propaganda western media
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This labels Western media as 'corporation propaganda,' explicitly framing it as a source of misinformation and manipulation, urging the audience to seek alternative information.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement evokes fear and fatalism about nuclear war, presenting it as an imminent or desirable outcome due to despair, which amplifies anxieties about catastrophic conflict.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: we deserve EVERYTHING we get
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This implies that Western populations are facing or will face severe negative consequences (presumably from the conflict) as a form of deserved punishment for their governments' actions.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: New Sarah Westall & Scott Ritter: Russia is Winning, Nuclear Threat is a Sign of Desperation & Very Serious
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The title makes a definitive claim about the war's outcome ('Russia is Winning') and speculates on the meaning behind nuclear threats, fitting the narrative of predicting or assuming conflict results.
